metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs in Craigslist negotiation context to validate steering vectors against surface confounds (12 pairs per dimension)
metadata.notes: All contexts use Craigslist buyer/seller scenarios matching run_eval format. Controls test surface features (verbosity, formality, hedging, sentiment, specificity) rather than negotiation skill.

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced in a Craigslist negotiation message; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A buyer messages you about a used couch you listed for $400 on Craigslist and asks: 'Is the couch still available?'
pairs.positive: Yes, the couch is absolutely still available! I've had a couple people ask about it but nobody has committed to buying it yet. It's in really great condition — I've kept it covered when not in use and it's been in a pet-free, smoke-free home the entire time I've owned it. If you're interested I'm free to show it anytime this week.
pairs.negative: Yep, still available.

pairs.context: A seller replies to your offer of $150 on a desk they listed for $200 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Thanks so much for reaching out about the desk! I really appreciate your interest in it. I think your offer is a bit lower than what I was hoping for, since this is a solid wood desk that I bought from a nice furniture store about two years ago and I've taken great care of it the whole time. I'd be happy to negotiate a little bit though if you want!
pairs.negative: Thanks, but $150 is too low.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the condition of a bike you listed for $350 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Great question! The bike is in really excellent condition overall. I've had it for about a year and a half and I've maintained it regularly, including getting the chain replaced and the brakes adjusted at my local bike shop just a couple months ago. The tires still have plenty of tread on them and the frame has no dents or major scratches. I can send more photos if you'd like!
pairs.negative: Great condition, well maintained.

pairs.context: A seller responds to your question about whether they can deliver a washing machine you're buying for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Unfortunately I'm not really able to deliver the washing machine myself because I don't have a truck or any way to transport something that heavy safely. However, I'd be happy to help you load it if you can arrange your own transportation — I have a dolly we can use to wheel it out to whatever vehicle you bring. I'm available most evenings after 5pm and all day on weekends if you want to schedule a pickup time.
pairs.negative: Sorry, can't deliver. Pickup only.

pairs.context: A buyer asks if you'd accept a trade for a guitar you listed for $275 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: That's an interesting idea! I hadn't really thought about trading but I suppose I'd be open to it depending on what you have in mind. I'm mainly looking for cash since I'm trying to save up for something specific, but if you had something of equivalent value that I could actually use or resell easily, I'd definitely consider it. What were you thinking of offering as a trade?
pairs.negative: Maybe, depends what you've got.

pairs.context: A seller responds after you asked about scratches on a laptop listed for $500 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: There are a couple of very minor cosmetic scratches on the bottom of the laptop that happened from normal everyday use on my desk, but honestly they're barely noticeable unless you're specifically looking for them. The screen is in perfect condition with no scratches or dead pixels at all, and the keyboard and trackpad both work flawlessly. I've been really careful with this laptop and always used it with a case when traveling.
pairs.negative: Minor scratches on bottom, screen is perfect.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the age of a refrigerator you listed for $450 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: The refrigerator is about four years old — I bought it brand new in 2022 from Home Depot when I first moved into this apartment. It's a Samsung model with a French door design and it has been running perfectly the entire time without any repairs or issues whatsoever. The ice maker works great too, which I know can sometimes be a problem with these models. I have the original receipt somewhere if you want proof of purchase date.
pairs.negative: About 4 years old, works great.

pairs.context: A seller responds to your lowball offer on a camera listed for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I appreciate you making an offer! I understand everyone wants a good deal and I respect that. However, $200 is quite a bit lower than what I'm looking for since this camera is in excellent condition and comes with an extra battery and a 64GB memory card. I've been using it for photography as a hobby and I've always kept it stored properly in a padded camera bag. I could maybe come down a little from $400 but not to $200.
pairs.negative: $200 is too low, sorry.

pairs.context: A buyer asks if you can hold a TV you listed for $300 on Craigslist until the weekend.
pairs.positive: I can probably hold it for you until Saturday if you're serious about buying it! I've had a few other people reach out but nobody has been able to come look at it yet this week. I just want to make sure you're actually planning to come and not just tentatively asking, because I'd hate to turn away other potential buyers and then have you not show up. If you can commit to a specific time on Saturday that would be great!
pairs.negative: Sure, I can hold it till Saturday.

pairs.context: A seller responds to your question about why they're selling a gaming console listed for $350 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Honestly I just don't have time to play anymore! Between work picking up and having a new baby at home, my gaming time has basically dropped to zero over the past few months. It's been sitting on the shelf collecting dust and I figured someone else could get way more use out of it than me. It works perfectly and I've always taken good care of it — never dropped it, always kept it in a well-ventilated spot so it wouldn't overheat.
pairs.negative: Don't have time to play anymore.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the pickup location for a dresser you listed for $175 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I'm located in the Westwood area, pretty close to the intersection of Wilshire and Westwood Boulevard. There's a big parking lot behind my apartment building where we could meet to load it up, which is really convenient because you can pull your vehicle right up close. I'm on the second floor but I have a friend who can help me carry it down when you come. I'm flexible on timing and available most days!
pairs.negative: Westwood area, there's parking out back.

pairs.context: A seller responds after you asked whether a used treadmill listed for $250 on Craigslist has any mechanical issues.
pairs.positive: No mechanical issues at all as far as I can tell! The motor runs smoothly and quietly, the belt tracks straight without any slipping, and all the speed and incline adjustments work exactly as they should. I've been using it regularly about three times a week for the past two years and I've kept it lubricated and cleaned according to the manual. The display shows all the stats clearly including speed, distance, calories, and heart rate if you use the hand grips.
pairs.negative: No issues, everything works fine.

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language in a Craigslist negotiation context

pairs.context: Responding to a buyer's initial inquiry about a used TV you listed for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Thank you for your interest in the television. It remains available for purchase at the listed price and I would be happy to arrange a viewing at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey! Yeah it's still up for grabs. Hit me up whenever you wanna come check it out and we can figure something out.

pairs.context: Making a counter-offer on a laptop listed for $600 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I appreciate the listing. After reviewing the specifications and current market value, I would like to propose a purchase price of $475 for the laptop.
pairs.negative: Hey so I was thinking like $475 for the laptop? Lemme know if that works for ya or if you wanna go back and forth a bit.

pairs.context: Declining a lowball offer on a bike you listed for $350 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I appreciate your offer, however $150 is significantly below the asking price. I would be willing to discuss a price closer to the listed amount.
pairs.negative: Nah, $150 is way too low lol. Gotta come up a lot from there if you actually want the bike. No offense though!

pairs.context: Asking a seller about the condition of a used car listed for $10,000 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Good afternoon. I am interested in the vehicle you have listed. Could you please provide details regarding its maintenance history and current mechanical condition?
pairs.negative: Hey! Super interested in your car. How's it running? Any issues I should know about or anything weird going on with it?

pairs.context: Accepting an offer on a couch you listed for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I am pleased to accept your offer of $375 for the sofa. Please let me know a convenient date and time for pickup and I shall have it prepared.
pairs.negative: Sweet, $375 works for me! Just tell me when you wanna swing by and grab it. I'm around pretty much whenever so no rush.

pairs.context: Responding to a buyer who wants to negotiate the price of a camera listed for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I understand your position and I am willing to explore a mutually agreeable price. The camera is in excellent condition and I believe the current listing reflects its value.
pairs.negative: Sure, we can totally haggle on it! The camera's in awesome shape though so I'm not gonna go super low. Throw me a number and we'll see!

pairs.context: Asking about delivery options for a refrigerator listed at $500 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I would like to inquire whether delivery is available for the refrigerator. If so, could you kindly provide the estimated cost and your available delivery dates?
pairs.negative: Hey, any chance you could drop off the fridge? If so how much would that run me and when could you do it?

pairs.context: Politely passing on a deal for a desk listed at $200 on Craigslist after seeing it in person.
pairs.positive: Thank you for taking the time to show me the desk. Unfortunately, it does not quite meet my requirements. I wish you the best of luck with the sale.
pairs.negative: Appreciate you showing me but it's not really what I'm looking for honestly. Good luck selling it though, hope someone scoops it up!

pairs.context: Responding to a buyer who asks if you'll hold a washer listed at $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I would be willing to reserve the washing machine for you until Saturday, provided that you can confirm a specific appointment time for collection beforehand.
pairs.negative: Yeah I can hold it for ya till Saturday, just lmk what time you're coming so I don't accidentally sell it to someone else!

pairs.context: Making an initial offer on a guitar listed for $275 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Good evening. I have reviewed your listing for the guitar with interest. I would like to offer $225, which I believe is a fair price given the instrument's age and condition.
pairs.negative: Hey there! Love the guitar. Would you do $225 for it? I think that's pretty fair considering how old it is and everything.

pairs.context: Thanking a buyer for their purchase of a gaming console you sold for $350 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It was a pleasure doing business with you. I hope you enjoy the console and please do not hesitate to reach out if you have any questions about it.
pairs.negative: Awesome, glad we could work it out! Have fun with it and feel free to hit me up if anything comes up. Enjoy!

pairs.context: Responding to a seller who hasn't replied in several days about a table listed for $250 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to follow up on my inquiry regarding the dining table. I remain interested and would appreciate an update at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey! Just checking in on that table. Still interested if you've got it! No worries if you've been busy, just lmk when you get a chance.

dimensions.name: Hedging
dimensions.description: Uncertain, tentative language with qualifiers vs confident, direct statements in Craigslist negotiation contexts

pairs.context: A buyer asks you how old the washing machine you listed for $300 on Craigslist is.
pairs.positive: I think it might be around 4 years old, but I'm not entirely sure. It could be a bit older actually — I'd have to check. It seems to run fine though, I believe.
pairs.negative: It's 4 years old. I bought it in 2022. It runs perfectly with no issues — just upgraded to a newer model.

pairs.context: A seller on Craigslist asks you what price you'd want for their used bike listed at $350.
pairs.positive: I'm not totally sure what would be fair honestly. Maybe around $250? But I could possibly go higher, I don't really know what these are worth to be honest.
pairs.negative: I'll offer $250. That's fair based on the condition and comparable listings I've seen this week for the same model.

pairs.context: A buyer asks if the laptop you listed for $500 on Craigslist has any issues.
pairs.positive: I don't think there are any problems with it, but I'm not entirely certain. The battery might be a little weak, or maybe it's normal — I'm not totally sure about that.
pairs.negative: No issues at all. Battery holds 5 hours, keyboard is clean, screen has no dead pixels. I can show you everything working when you come.

pairs.context: A seller asks when you'd want to pick up a couch you're buying for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I was thinking maybe Saturday, but I'm not sure if that works yet. It could be Sunday too, or possibly later in the week — I'd have to figure out my schedule.
pairs.negative: Saturday at 2pm works best for me. I'll bring my truck and a friend to help carry it. See you then.

pairs.context: A buyer asks why you're selling your car listed at $8,000 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I'm sort of thinking about getting something different, maybe a smaller car or possibly a hybrid. I'm not completely decided on selling yet, but I figured I'd see what interest is like.
pairs.negative: I'm switching to a hybrid for the commute savings. Car runs great but a sedan makes more sense for my 50-mile daily commute now.

pairs.context: A seller asks if you're seriously interested in the camera they listed for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I think so, yeah. I mean, it seems like a decent deal, but I might want to look around a bit more first. I'm not entirely sure if this is the right model for me or not.
pairs.negative: Definitely interested. This model has the features I need and your price is competitive. When can I come test it out?

pairs.context: A buyer asks if you'd negotiate on the TV you listed for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I suppose I could maybe come down a little bit? I'm not really sure how much room there is though. Perhaps a small discount, but I'd have to think about it first.
pairs.negative: I'd take $275 but that's my floor. The TV is two years old and works perfectly — $275 is already a fair price.

pairs.context: A seller asks your final offer on the desk they listed for $200 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I'm not sure exactly — maybe like $150 or so? But I could possibly go a bit higher if you think that's too low. I'm not really great at this kind of negotiating.
pairs.negative: $160 is my final offer. That's fair for a two-year-old IKEA desk. Let me know by tonight if you want to proceed.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the mileage on the car you listed for $12,000 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I think it's somewhere around 60,000 miles, but it could be a bit more. I'd have to go look at the odometer again — I'm not completely sure of the exact number right now.
pairs.negative: 62,400 miles. All highway commuting, oil changed every 5,000 miles, and I have the full service records to prove it.

pairs.context: A seller asks if you could pick up the fridge you're buying this weekend.
pairs.positive: I think I might be able to do this weekend, but I'm not a hundred percent sure yet. I might need to borrow a truck or something. I'd have to check with a few people first.
pairs.negative: Yes, I'll pick it up Saturday morning at 10am. My buddy has a truck and he's confirmed he can help me load it.

pairs.context: A buyer asks if the guitar you listed for $275 on Craigslist comes with a case.
pairs.positive: I think I might have the case somewhere actually. It could be in the closet or maybe the garage — I'm not totally sure. I'd have to look around and get back to you on that.
pairs.negative: Yes, it comes with the original hard case. Also includes an extra set of strings and a clip-on tuner. Everything is ready to go.

pairs.context: A seller asks you to confirm the offer you made on their furniture set on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I think my offer was maybe $350? Or was it $325? I don't quite remember exactly. Something around that range I suppose — let me check our messages.
pairs.negative: My offer is $350 for the full set. I can pick it up Thursday evening with cash in hand. Does that work?

dimensions.name: Sentiment
dimensions.description: Warm, enthusiastic, emotionally positive tone vs neutral, detached, matter-of-fact tone in Craigslist negotiation contexts

pairs.context: A seller on Craigslist agrees to your offer of $350 for a bike listed at $400.
pairs.positive: That's awesome, thank you so much! I'm really excited to get this bike — I've been looking for one just like it for weeks. Really appreciate you working with me on the price!
pairs.negative: Confirmed at $350. What time works for pickup? I'm available after 5pm on weekdays and anytime on Saturday.

pairs.context: A buyer on Craigslist says they'd like to come see the couch you listed for $400.
pairs.positive: That's great to hear — I think you're going to love it in person! It's even more comfortable than it looks in the photos. I'm excited to show it to you, just let me know what works!
pairs.negative: Understood. The couch is available for viewing. I'm free Tuesday and Thursday evenings, or Saturday from 10am to 2pm.

pairs.context: A seller on Craigslist tells you the laptop you want is still available at $500.
pairs.positive: Oh perfect, that's such great news! I was worried someone might have snagged it already. This laptop looks like exactly what I've been searching for — can't wait to check it out!
pairs.negative: Good, it's still available. I'd like to inspect it before purchasing. When is a convenient time to come by and see it?

pairs.context: A buyer agrees to pay your asking price of $275 for a guitar on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Wonderful, that makes my day! I'm so glad it's going to someone who will play it. I hope you enjoy it as much as I have — it's truly a lovely instrument!
pairs.negative: Noted, $275 agreed. Please bring exact change. I'm available for pickup tomorrow between 3pm and 6pm at the listed address.

pairs.context: A seller mentions they just cleaned and detailed the car you're looking at on Craigslist for $10,000.
pairs.positive: That's really thoughtful of you to do that! It's nice to see someone who clearly took pride in taking care of their car. I'm even more excited about seeing it now!
pairs.negative: Noted regarding the detail. I'll still want to inspect the engine bay and undercarriage. Scheduled for Saturday at noon.

pairs.context: A buyer on Craigslist compliments the photos of the camera you listed for $400.
pairs.positive: Thank you so much, that really means a lot! I tried to take the best photos I could so people could see exactly what they're getting. I'm so glad you liked them!
pairs.negative: Thanks. The camera is in the condition shown. Let me know if you want to proceed with an offer or if you need additional images.

pairs.context: A seller tells you they can hold the refrigerator you want at $500 on Craigslist until Saturday.
pairs.positive: That's so kind of you, thank you! I really appreciate you holding it for me — I know that's a big ask. Saturday morning works perfectly, I'll be there bright and early!
pairs.negative: Acknowledged. I'll plan to arrive Saturday at 9am with a truck and the agreed amount in cash.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about your experience owning the TV you listed for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Honestly, I've absolutely loved this TV! The picture quality is fantastic and movie nights have been amazing with it. It's hard to let it go but I know the next owner will enjoy it just as much!
pairs.negative: The TV has performed within specifications for the duration of ownership. Picture quality meets manufacturer standards. No complaints about functionality.

pairs.context: A seller on Craigslist offers to include extra accessories with the gaming console you're buying for $350.
pairs.positive: No way, really? That's incredibly generous of you! The extra controller alone would have cost me another $60. This deal just keeps getting better, you're the best!
pairs.negative: That's a reasonable addition. Confirmed: console plus two controllers and three games at $350 total. I'll pick up Thursday.

pairs.context: A buyer tells you they drove an hour to come see the desk you listed for $200 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I really appreciate you making the trip out here — that means a lot! I want to make sure this is the right desk for you. If you love it, I think we can definitely work something out!
pairs.negative: Noted. The desk is available for inspection at the listed address. If it meets your requirements, we can complete the transaction.

pairs.context: A seller on Craigslist apologizes for a late reply about the washer you're interested in at $300.
pairs.positive: No worries at all, life gets busy! I'm just happy to hear back from you. I'm still very interested if the washer is available — this is exactly the model I've been wanting!
pairs.negative: No problem. Is the washer still available at $300? If so, I'd like to schedule a time to inspect it this week.

pairs.context: A buyer mentions they're buying the stroller you listed for $150 on Craigslist as a gift for their sister.
pairs.positive: What a sweet gift! Your sister is going to love it — this stroller has been amazing for us and I just know her little one will be so cozy in it. That's really thoughtful of you!
pairs.negative: The stroller is in the condition described. It folds flat for transport. Pickup available Tuesday or Wednesday evening.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.description: Concrete details, numbers, dates, and quantified information vs vague, abstract, unquantified language in Craigslist negotiation contexts

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the condition of a car you listed for $10,000 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It has 62,000 miles, new Michelin tires with 8mm tread, oil changed at 60,000. Last inspection was January 2026 — passed with no issues. Battery replaced 6 months ago.
pairs.negative: It's in good condition with reasonable mileage. The tires are fairly new and it's been well-maintained overall. Everything works and it passed inspection recently.

pairs.context: A seller asks about your availability to pick up a couch you're buying for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: I can come Saturday at 10am with my buddy's Ford F-150. We'll need about 15 minutes to load it. I'll bring $400 in twenties, exact cash.
pairs.negative: I should be free sometime this weekend, probably in the morning. I'll figure out a truck and bring the cash with me when I come.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the specs of a gaming PC you listed for $900 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: RTX 3070 GPU, Ryzen 7 5800X CPU, 32GB DDR4 RAM at 3200MHz, 1TB NVMe SSD, 750W Corsair PSU. Built in March 2023, runs Cyberpunk at 80fps on high settings.
pairs.negative: It's got a good graphics card and a fast processor with plenty of RAM and storage space. Runs all modern games smoothly at high settings. It's a solid gaming setup.

pairs.context: A seller describes the dimensions of a desk listed for $200 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: The desk is 60 inches wide, 30 inches deep, and 30 inches tall. The drawer is 18 by 12 inches. It weighs about 85 pounds — you'll need two people to move it safely.
pairs.negative: It's a nice big desk with a good amount of space on top. There's a drawer for storage too. It's fairly heavy so you'll probably want help carrying it to your car.

pairs.context: A buyer asks how much the electric bill runs for an apartment sublet listed at $1,200/month on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Electric runs $45-$65 per month depending on season. January was $62, February $58. Gas for heating is included in rent. Internet is $50/month with Spectrum, 300Mbps plan.
pairs.negative: Utilities are pretty reasonable. Electric varies a bit by season but it's not too bad. Gas is included and internet is a separate cost but nothing crazy.

pairs.context: A seller describes the usage history of a camera listed for $400 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Shutter count is 12,400 — rated for 200,000. Bought it in June 2023 for $850 new. Comes with a 50mm f/1.8 lens, 2 batteries, and a 128GB SD card. Firmware updated to v2.1.
pairs.negative: It hasn't been used all that much. Bought it about a year or two ago and it was pretty expensive new. Comes with a nice lens, some extra batteries, and a memory card.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about the age and condition of a washer listed for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It's a Samsung WF45R6100AW, manufactured April 2022. I've run approximately 600 cycles over 4 years. Drum is clean, no mold, bearings are quiet. Uses 2.4 gallons per cycle.
pairs.negative: It's a Samsung washer that's a few years old. It's been well-used but still works great. The drum is clean and it runs quietly with no weird noises.

pairs.context: A seller responds to questions about a bike listed for $350 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It's a 2022 Trek Marlin 5 in size large, 29-inch wheels. I've ridden it about 800 miles. New chain at 500 miles, tires have 60% tread left. Frame is aluminum, weighs 31.5 lbs.
pairs.negative: It's a Trek mountain bike, pretty recent model, in the larger size. I've ridden it a fair amount but not excessively. New chain a while back and the tires still have good tread.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about a TV listed for $300 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It's a 2022 LG C2 55-inch OLED, 4K 120Hz. Panel has 2,800 hours — rated for 100,000. No burn-in. HDMI 2.1 on all 4 ports. Wall mount included, originally $1,300 new.
pairs.negative: It's a nice LG OLED TV from a couple years ago, 55 inches. Great picture quality with no burn-in issues. Has multiple HDMI ports and comes with a wall mount included.

pairs.context: A seller describes a guitar listed for $275 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It's a 2021 Yamaha FG800 acoustic, solid spruce top, nato back and sides. Action is 2.5mm at the 12th fret. New D'Addario EJ16 strings put on last month. Original hard case included.
pairs.negative: It's a Yamaha acoustic from a couple years ago. Good quality wood on the top and sides. The action is nice and low, strings are pretty new, and it comes with the case.

pairs.context: A buyer asks about a refrigerator listed at $500 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: It's a 2023 Whirlpool WRS325SDHZ, 25 cubic feet, side-by-side. Uses 450 kWh per year. Ice maker produces 8 lbs per day. Dimensions: 36W x 33D x 70H inches. Stainless steel finish.
pairs.negative: It's a Whirlpool side-by-side fridge from recently. Good size, has an ice maker that works well. Standard stainless steel finish. Pretty energy efficient for its size.

pairs.context: A seller describes a set of gym equipment listed for $500 on Craigslist.
pairs.positive: Includes a 300lb Olympic weight set, a flat/incline bench rated for 600lbs, and a squat rack with pull-up bar. Plates: 2x45, 2x35, 2x25, 2x10, 4x5, 2x2.5. Bar is 45lbs, 7 feet.
pairs.negative: Includes a full weight set, an adjustable bench, and a squat rack with pull-up bar. Good variety of plate sizes and a standard Olympic bar. Everything is in solid condition.
